item.text: {"case_id":"mtj-003","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The assistant does not directly list a migration checklist. It first reframes the premise, saying not to migrate just because SPA is considered outdated, and identifies that SSR serves concrete goals such as first-screen performance, SEO, sharing previews, indexing, compliance, and weak-device experience. It asks for the target function and constraints, including page type, SEO/public content, Web Vitals, current stack, backend capacity, browser dependencies, and team ability to maintain SSR complexity. Only after narrowing the decision does it provide a phased mixed-rendering migration plan. This matches the expected input-framing audit behavior."}
